de.uka.ilkd.key.proof

Class PrefixTermTacletAppIndexCacheImpl

  • java.lang.Object
    • de.uka.ilkd.key.proof.PrefixTermTacletAppIndexCache
      • de.uka.ilkd.key.proof.PrefixTermTacletAppIndexCacheImpl
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    ITermTacletAppIndexCache
    Direct Known Subclasses:
    TermTacletAppIndexCacheSet.BelowProgCache, TermTacletAppIndexCacheSet.TopLevelCache

    public abstract class PrefixTermTacletAppIndexCacheImpl
extends PrefixTermTacletAppIndexCache
    The abstract superclass of caches for taclet app indexes that are implemented using a common backend LRUCache (the backend is stored in TermTacletAppIndexCacheSet). The backend is accessed in a way that guarantees that two distinct instances of this class never interfere, by choosing cache keys that are specific for a particular instance of PrefixTermTacletAppIndexCacheImpl and cannot be created by other instances. This ensures that it is safe to use one instance of LRUCache for many instances of PrefixTermTacletAppIndexCacheImpl (different proofs, different proof branches, different locations).

    • Method Detail

      • getIndexForTerm

        public TermTacletAppIndex getIndexForTerm(Term t)
        Returns:
        the taclet app index for the term t, or null if no index for this term was found in the cache

      • countAccess

        private void countAccess(boolean hit)

      • putIndexForTerm

        public void putIndexForTerm(Term t,
                   TermTacletAppIndex index)

        Put the taclet app index index for the term t in the cache

      • name

        protected abstract String name()
        Only used for debugging purposes

      • getNewKey

        private PrefixTermTacletAppIndexCacheImpl.CacheKey getNewKey(Term t)
        Returns:
        a freshly created key for the term t that can be stored in the cache

      • getQueryKey

        private PrefixTermTacletAppIndexCacheImpl.CacheKey getQueryKey(Term t)
        Returns:
        a key for the term t that can be used for cache queries. Calling this method twice will return the same object (with different attribute values), i.e., the result is not supposed to be stored anywhere
